skills.title: TanStack Fullstack Pattern
skills.summary: Apply the TanStack Start fullstack architectural pattern with interface-first boundaries for repository, AI, and observability services.
skills.projectSummary: A production-ready TanStack Start template designed to make internal tools AI promptable by default.

supportedTools.name: Windsurf
supportedTools.notes: Reads `.agents/skills/` directly when the skill lives in the project.

supportedTools.name: Cursor
supportedTools.notes: Copy the generated skill folder into the global Cursor skills directory.

supportedTools.name: Claude Code
supportedTools.notes: Copy the generated skill folder into the Claude Code skills directory.

capabilities: Natural language querying through repository-backed AI tools
URL-aware AI prompt context using current location and route patterns
Swappable service implementations behind stable interfaces
Layer-specific schemas with explicit mapping between repository and tool contracts
inputs: Existing or new TanStack Start codebase
Domain requirements for data model and auth
outputs: Loader-first routes and URL-driven state conventions
constraints: External services are accessed through interfaces only
Domain types come from schemas and inferred types
Repository and tools use separate schemas with explicit mapping between layers
AI and UI consume tools-layer schemas only, never repository schemas directly
Route data fetching is done in loaders, not effects
Mutations use invalidation middleware and normalized responses
steps: Define schemas as source of truth and infer types
Split schemas by layer: repository input/output schemas and tool-facing schemas
Define repository interfaces and provide seed + real implementations
Map repository inputs/outputs to tool schemas at the tools boundary
Add server functions with GET query and POST mutation conventions
Expose read repository methods as safe AI tools
Keep route state in URL search params with validateSearch

examples.input: Add a new domain entity to the template
examples.output: Update schema, repository interfaces, server functions, routes, and AI tools
Implement repository interface and switch factory without app-level changes
